talk_metadata.talk_title: Spatial Transcriptomic Profiling of Colorectal Cancer Tumors in Alaska Native People: Discovery of Prognostic Biomarkers

key_data_gaps: Additional 100 patients' data being generated for geomix will be included
Other spatial proteomics and transcriptomics data from sequential TMA slides to drive more effective and complete prognostic biomarker sets

unresolved_questions: Do you need spatial transcriptomics for this kind of biomarkers? Would you be able to have a pathologist annotate the regions and use some tools to isolate and sequence these separately in bulk transcriptomics?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Highly Multiplexed Immunoassays That Enable the Discovery of Multi-Marker EV Surface Signatures as Novel Biomarkers

key_data_gaps: Validation of prognostic value in larger cohorts
Further investigation into early detection potential

crispro_opportunity.description: The ability to detect multi-marker EV surface signatures offers a new dimension for analyzing liquid biopsies and capturing tumor ecosystem complexity that single analyses cannot resolve.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So overall, we've put together a multimarker profiling technique that we think opens new dimensions for analyzing liquid biopsies. It helps to capture the tumor ecosystem complexity in a way that single analyses can't resolve.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational spine can analyze complex multi-marker signatures from EV surface data to identify novel patient stratification biomarkers.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: check for ongoing trials utilizing EV surface markers for patient stratification
PubMed: research on specific EV surface marker combinations and their correlation with treatment response

crispro_opportunity.description: The technology is being expanded to other cancer types beyond colorectal cancer, with potential to decode the composition of the tumor microenvironment based on circulating EVs.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So we've started working with a few different cancer types, not just colorectal cancer. We're also interested in expanding our library of antibodies to look at the broader tumor microenvironment to kind of decode the composition of that microenvironment based on the circulating EVs.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate EV surface signature data with other multi-omic data to provide a comprehensive understanding of the tumor microenvironment across various cancer types.
external_validation_needed: Pipeline databases (Citeline, Evaluate): track development of EV-based diagnostics for other cancer types
Company websites: monitor Mesoscale Diagnostics' progress in expanding their antibody library and assay capabilities

unresolved_questions: What are the plans for validation of these multi-marker EV surface signatures?
How will the technology be translated into a clinical assay?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Differences in Survival Outcomes Among Metastatic Colorectal Cancer Patients Across Fibroblast Growth Factor Receptor Two and Three Expression Levels

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: The role of FGFR2 in angiogenesis was hypothesized to be linked to worse survival, but it was not predictive of overall survival in patients treated with bevacizumab, an anti-angiogenic drug.

key_data_gaps: Further investigation into the mechanisms underlying the differential behavior of FGFR2 and FGFR3.
Exploration of FGFR3 SNPs in other cohorts.
Pre-clinical validation of the role of FGFR2 and FGFR3 in cetuximab response.

unresolved_questions: Why do FGFR2 and FGFR3 behave differently, given they are from the same family?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Mapping of Spatial Organization of B Cells and Immune Signaling in Triple Negative Breast Cancer to Characterize Response to Immunotherapy

key_data_gaps: Exploration of antibodies produced by plasma cells.
Analysis of plasma samples for secreted antibodies.
B cell receptor sequencing.
Validation of findings in larger cohorts.

crispro_opportunity.description: The spatial organization of B cells and their interactions within niches are predictive of immunotherapy response in triple-negative breast cancer, suggesting a gap in current predictive models that primarily focus on T cells or PD-L1.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So taken together, these results from niches, their interactions suggest what drives response to immunotherapy is not just the abundance of B cells but also measures of their spatial organization.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can leverage spatial multi-omic data to identify and validate B cell-centric biomarkers for immunotherapy response prediction in TNBC.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: search for ongoing TNBC immunotherapy trials with available spatial multi-omic data
PubMed: research on B cell heterogeneity and function in TNBC immunotherapy response

crispro_opportunity.description: The presence of B cell niches and their interactions, particularly the B activated niche, are associated with immunotherapy response, suggesting that targeting B cell pathways could enhance immunotherapy efficacy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: That specifically the B cell activation signature remains significant across spatial scales, and its interactions are also having predictive capacity for immunotherapy response.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the molecular pathways within B cell niches to identify potential combination targets that synergize with immunotherapy.
external_validation_needed: Drug databases: identify existing drugs targeting B cell pathways
Preclinical models: test combinations of immunotherapy with B cell-targeting agents in TNBC models

unresolved_questions: Were you surprised to think of plasma cells and B cells as immune reactive and being responsible for this, given that people usually focus on T cells or other cells?
Have you looked at antibodies at all in what these plasma cells are producing?
Have you looked at the plasma of those same patients with secret antibodies?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AI-Powered Deep Visual Proteomics, DVP, Links Pancreatic Precursor Biology to PDAC Survival Phenotypes

key_data_gaps: Clinical validation of identified biomarkers.
Development of blood-based assays for early detection and monitoring.
Further investigation into the role of immune editing and evasion in early pancreatic cancer progression.

crispro_opportunity.description: Deep Visual Proteomics can identify early molecular changes in pancreatic precursor lesions and normal ducts associated with cancer development, offering a potential for early detection and stratification.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: And then we had another cohort, which is the cancer-associated cohort, where we isolated cancer-associated normal ducts, low-grade and very rare high-grade PANAs, and the tumor cells.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate DVP-generated proteomic data with genomic and clinical data to build predictive models for PDAC risk stratification and early detection.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: search for ongoing PDAC early detection trials
PubMed: research on early molecular markers of PDAC
Company pipelines: track development of novel PDAC diagnostic tools

crispro_opportunity.description: The ability to combine genotype with phenotype, including KRAS mutations, using DVP, could help in understanding resistance mechanisms to targeted therapies and potentially identify salvage strategies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: And just to give you an idea, what we found is that when we look at the ADMs in low-grade, we can see in a number of those hotspot mutations, when we look on the patient level, we can see that some patients have one mutation, others have several different mutations in the different regions of interest.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze DVP data to identify specific KRAS mutation profiles associated with treatment resistance and suggest combination therapies or alternative treatment strategies.
external_validation_needed: Drug databases: identify KRAS-targeted therapies and their resistance mechanisms
Clinical trial data: analyze outcomes of patients with specific KRAS mutations treated with various therapies

unresolved_questions: Can this technology become a diagnostic test that we can use in the clinic?
Is there a way to see this in a blood-based rather than tissue-based proteomics?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland
talk_metadata.talk_title: Spatial Immune Multiomics Refines Prognostication in Early-Stage Estrogen Receptor-Positive Breast Cancer

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Inflamed TME and epithelia in ER+ breast cancer appear dysfunctional, upregulating checkpoints, downregulating estrogen receptor, and suppressing DNA repair pathways, leading to chemo inferiority in patients with high stromal CD8 density.

key_data_gaps: External validation in larger TRX before generalization to practice.
Further investigation into the relationship between ER signaling and immune responses.
Exploration of the role of chemotherapy in inducing ovarian function suppression and its impact on immune responses.

crispro_opportunity.description: High stromal CD8 density in ER+ breast cancer is a predictive marker of chemo inferiority and adds prognostic information to clinical variables, suggesting a need for improved patient stratification beyond current genomic assays.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: But more crucially is that the inflamed milieu, particularly those with high stromal CD8, at least in alcohol, is a predictive marker of chemo inferiority. It does add significant prognostic information to clinical variables, including menopausal status.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate spatial immune multi-omic data with genomic and clinical data to develop a refined prognostic model for ER+ breast cancer, identifying patients who may not benefit from chemotherapy.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: search for ongoing ER+ breast cancer trials with available spatial multi-omic data
PubMed: research on immune microenvironment in ER+ breast cancer and its impact on treatment response

crispro_opportunity.description: The identification of dysfunctional immune states in ER+ breast cancer, including checkpoint upregulation and M2-like macrophage signatures, suggests potential for combination therapies with immunotherapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: The inflamed states do appear transcriptomically wired to more dysfunctions, such as the expression of checkpoints, exhaustion transcripts, M2-like macrophage, and remodeling genes, as well as dedifferentiation, potentially from luminal biology.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the molecular pathways within these dysfunctional immune states to identify novel combination targets that could synergize with immunotherapy in ER+ breast cancer.
external_validation_needed: Drug databases: identify existing immunotherapies and agents targeting checkpoints or M2 macrophages
Preclinical models: test combinations of immunotherapy with other agents in ER+ breast cancer models

cited_competitors.name: Oncotype DX Recurrence Score
cited_competitors.context: Used as a benchmark for risk stratification, but has limitations in the intermediate risk group.

unresolved_questions: How can this biomarker be translated into the clinic?
Would checkpoint inhibitors be considered in these patients?
How would this biomarker correlate in triple-negative breast cancer?
Could there be an interaction with estrogen receptor signaling?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Unbiased Phenotyping of CRISPR Edited Tumors with Preserved Map and Subcellular Imaging of the Mouse Whole Transcriptome, 22,000 Plex

key_data_gaps: Validation of humanized mouse models with actual human cell xenografts.
Further investigation into the specific murine genes that correlate with human gene expression.
Clinical translation of identified combination targets and biomarkers.

crispro_opportunity.description: The platform allows for the identification of novel combination targets by analyzing the phenotypic shifts of CRISPR-edited tumors upon therapeutic intervention, such as identifying a rationale for combining VEGF pathway inhibitors with PD1 blockade.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: And so what becomes interesting is that we can look at the trajectories of these different knockouts upon treatment with different drugs. And for example, if you here look at this knockout of VGFR1, the VGFR receptor, when you treat this knockout with PD1, you see a drastic shift towards the right of the space of the phenotype of this tumor. So these tumors under PD1 become tumors that have a very hot TME.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can leverage the BERTA platform to screen genetic perturbations and therapeutic combinations, identifying synergistic targets and predicting optimal therapeutic strategies.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: search for ongoing trials investigating VEGF inhibitors and PD1 blockade combinations
PubMed: research on the role of VEGFR1 in TME modulation and response to immunotherapy

crispro_opportunity.description: The ability to project human biology onto mouse models allows for the identification of human-specific biomarkers, such as CCAM6, in preclinical settings, which can then be used for patient stratification.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: And what we find that we look at the knockouts, it's a knockout of a transcription factor that essentially is going to induce a switch in histology of the tumor. And the resulting histology resembles the tumor histology in patients that have high CCAM6 expression.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can utilize the humanization capability of the BERTA platform to identify and validate human biomarkers in preclinical models, facilitating their translation to clinical applications.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: research on CCAM6 expression in human lung cancer and its prognostic significance
Company pipelines: track development of CCAM6-targeted therapies or diagnostics

unresolved_questions: Is the gene prediction, gene expression prediction based on the cellular level or regional level?
How can you validate your prediction?
Have you looked at the attention maps or looked at correlated genes that maybe CCAM6 wasn't the driver, but there is a correlated murine gene with CCAM6 that could explain what you're seeing?
Does MAIRS work as well if you inject human cells into mouse, so you have both mouse and human cells?
Is it available for researchers?
How many genes can we identify in the inferred spatial transcriptomes?
What if you have in vivo data? So let's say you inject some human cells into mouse, so you have both mouse and human cells. Does MAIRS work as well?
How do you think the problem of mapping one human to another human might apply across people, given that humans also have genetic variations and compensatory mechanisms?
